title: eremophila forrestii
intro: Eremophila forrestii, a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, is also known as Forrest's emu bush. This Australian native plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Eremophila forrestii include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats. Eremophila forrestii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
